metadata.title: Sintesis Workflow Map
metadata.description: Data-driven map of how app packages, components, APIs, and services collaborate to complete product and build workflows.

nodes.label: Browser / User
nodes.summary: Clicks, form submissions, uploads, route navigation, and build commands initiated by a user or developer.

nodes.label: app/
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router pages, layouts, route groups, and server components.

nodes.label: app/admin
nodes.summary: Tenant-scoped administration for users, roles, defaults, audit log, secrets, and configuration.

nodes.label: app/excel
nodes.summary: Main obra workspace: General, Documents, Tablas, Flujo, and data-flow experiences.

nodes.label: app/macro
nodes.summary: Cross-obra macro table views and report entry points.

nodes.label: app/document-generation
nodes.summary: Document template selection, draft data entry, and generated document review.

nodes.label: app/notifications
nodes.summary: Inbox view that merges pendientes, calendar events, workflow items, and notification records.

nodes.label: components/
nodes.summary: Shared React components, app shell, tabs, viewers, reports, quick actions, and shadcn primitives.

nodes.label: components/form-table
nodes.summary: Editable table engine used for obras, certificates, dynamic tablas, and admin data grids.

nodes.label: components/quick-actions
nodes.summary: Floating multi-step action runner configured from tenant default folders and tables.

nodes.label: components/viewer + report
nodes.summary: Document preview, PDF/3D viewers, report builders, and rendered report surfaces.

nodes.label: app/api
nodes.summary: Route handlers for obras, tablas, OCR imports, macros, calendars, notifications, billing, and PDF rendering.

nodes.label: lib auth + tenant
nodes.summary: Resolves the active tenant, user roles, route visibility, and request authorization.

nodes.label: lib/obra-defaults
nodes.summary: Materializes tenant default folders, tabs, tables, and action scaffolding into an obra instance.

nodes.label: lib/tablas
nodes.summary: Dynamic table schema helpers, parsing, coercion, formula support, and row handling contracts.

nodes.label: lib/data-flow-*
nodes.summary: Effective data-flow config, calculations, traceability, writeback suggestions, and recomputation helpers.

nodes.label: lib/macro-tables
nodes.summary: Cross-obra source selection, stable identity, override mapping, and macro row assembly.

nodes.label: lib/notifications + workflow
nodes.summary: Notification rules, delivery engine, workflow helpers, emails, and calendar side-effect orchestration.

nodes.label: lib/email + Resend
nodes.summary: Transactional email templates and Resend delivery adapter for invitations and workflow notifications.

nodes.label: Supabase Postgres + RLS
nodes.summary: Tenant-scoped relational data, RPC functions, auth identities, memberships, and RLS policies.

nodes.label: Supabase Storage
nodes.summary: Document binaries, generated files, PDFs, CSV/XLSX uploads, and model artifacts.

nodes.label: AI OCR / extraction
nodes.summary: Structured extraction from PDFs, images, spreadsheets, and invoices into tenant-configured tables.

nodes.label: workflow engine
nodes.summary: Async jobs for reminders, obra completion, notifications, and scheduled dispatches.

nodes.label: scripts/
nodes.summary: Demo tenant bootstrapping, backfills, migrations support, and local operational scripts.

nodes.label: tests/
nodes.summary: Vitest unit coverage and Playwright route/workflow checks.

nodes.label: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js build behavior, Sentry wrapping, server external packages, and image/runtime config.

nodes.label: .next / deployment bundle
nodes.summary: Compiled Next.js server/client output used by deployment and desktop packaging flows.

nodes.label: ToDesktop / desktop shell
nodes.summary: External desktop packaging layer. This repo exposes the Next build artifact, but no ToDesktop config was found here.

nodes.label: remotion/
nodes.summary: Marketing/product video compositions and rendering support.

flows.label: Invite new user
flows.summary: Tenant admin creates an invitation, the system sends an email token, and acceptance creates tenant membership.

steps.label: Open Users admin
steps.passes: tenant context, invite form values
steps.notes: The admin UI scopes the action to the active tenant.

steps.label: Create invitation
steps.passes: email, invited_role, active tenant id
steps.notes: Server action/API validates admin capability and duplicate pending invites.

steps.label: Authorize
steps.passes: Supabase session, tenant membership, role checks
steps.notes: Tenant and role resolution keep UI permissions and server writes aligned.

steps.label: Persist token
steps.passes: invitations row, status=pending, expiry, invited role
steps.notes: Database constraints prevent duplicate active invitations.

steps.label: Send invite email
steps.passes: recipient email, tenant name, invitation token link
steps.notes: Resend delivery is best-effort; the DB invite remains the source of truth.

steps.label: Accept token link
steps.passes: token from /invitations/[token]
steps.notes: Public token lookup is allowed through security-definer database helpers.

steps.label: Create membership
steps.passes: tenant_id, user_id, invited_role, accepted_at
steps.notes: Acceptance updates invitation status and makes route guards recognize the new member.

flows.label: Create obra
flows.summary: A new obra row is created, then tenant defaults materialize folders, tablas, and workflow-ready structure.

steps.label: Submit obra row
steps.passes: obra fields and configured FormTable columns
steps.notes: The Excel panel owns the spreadsheet-like creation surface.

steps.label: Normalize table edit
steps.passes: column config, cell values, validation state
steps.notes: FormTable converts user edits into the API payload shape.

steps.label: PATCH /api/obras/bulk
steps.passes: tenant-scoped obra payload
steps.notes: The route handler enforces tenant membership before persistence.

steps.label: Apply defaults
steps.passes: tenant template, obra id, folders, tabs, table definitions
steps.notes: Defaults seed downstream OCR, quick actions, and reporting structure.

steps.label: Persist obra graph
steps.passes: obra, folder rows, tabla rows, columns, config JSON
steps.notes: Database state becomes the source for Documents, Tablas, General, and Macro surfaces.

flows.label: Upload document + OCR
flows.summary: A document upload lands in storage, extraction maps it to configured table rows, and lineage-aware consumers refresh.

steps.label: Drop file in Documents
steps.passes: file binary, obra id, target folder
steps.notes: The Documents tab routes uploads through the obra file manager.

steps.label: Store binary
steps.passes: tenant/obra storage path and file metadata
steps.notes: Supabase Storage holds the document bytes.

steps.label: Run import route
steps.passes: folder config, tabla id, document pointer
steps.notes: The API chooses OCR, spreadsheet, or manual import logic based on configured defaults.

steps.label: Extract structure
steps.passes: document content, Zod schema, extraction instructions
steps.notes: AI returns rows that match tenant table columns.

steps.label: Coerce and lineage
steps.passes: extracted values, column types, fingerprints, lineage row keys
steps.notes: Table helpers normalize types and preserve stable business identity across reimports.

steps.label: Write rows
steps.passes: tabla rows, cells, document links, extraction metadata
steps.notes: Persisted rows feed macro tables, data-flow, reports, and notifications.

steps.label: Refresh consumers
steps.passes: changed source rows and recalculation inputs
steps.notes: Derived indicators can recompute or surface writeback suggestions.

flows.label: Run quick action
flows.summary: A tenant-configured action walks the user through upload, OCR import, or manual row entry steps for an obra.

steps.label: Render panel
steps.passes: obra defaults, action definitions, folder nature
steps.notes: Quick actions appear on the Obra General tab.

steps.label: Execute step
steps.passes: current step id, uploaded file or row payload
steps.notes: Each step delegates to the same document/table APIs used by the full tabs.

steps.label: Optional file write
steps.passes: binary, folder path, file metadata
steps.notes: Upload steps persist documents before import.

steps.label: Optional row import
steps.passes: manual values or OCR output
steps.notes: Manual and OCR steps converge into table rows.

steps.label: Persist progress
steps.passes: rows, action completion state, document links
steps.notes: The panel refreshes Documents and General data after each step.

flows.label: Recompute data-flow
flows.summary: Tenant and obra data-flow configs merge, sources evaluate, results render, and writeback suggestions can update obra fields.

steps.label: Load data-flow config
steps.passes: obra id, tenant id, selected General/Data Flow view
steps.notes: Routes read inherited tenant config and obra-local overrides.

steps.label: Merge + evaluate
steps.passes: sources, calculations, results, layout blocks
steps.notes: Effective config combines tenant defaults with obra-specific changes.

steps.label: Read table sources
steps.passes: source table ids, columns, aggregates, filters
steps.notes: Table and macro inputs become calculation variables.

steps.label: Read macro sources
steps.passes: macro table selection and override state
steps.notes: Macro tables can participate as source nodes.

steps.label: Render General layout
steps.passes: evaluated results, status, formatted values, traceability ids
steps.notes: Cards and traceability canvas share the same evaluated output.

steps.label: Optional writeback
steps.passes: suggested or automatic obra custom_data patch
steps.notes: Writeback preserves auditability and blocks unsafe internal fields.

flows.label: Build macro table
flows.summary: Macro views aggregate configured source tablas across obras, preserving stable row identities and user overrides.

steps.label: Open macro view
steps.passes: selected macro table, filters, tenant context
steps.notes: The macro route is the cross-obra reporting surface.

steps.label: Fetch rows
steps.passes: macro id, source selections, pagination/filter state
steps.notes: API handlers centralize source resolution and override merge behavior.

steps.label: Resolve sources
steps.passes: source tabla ids, selected obras, column mapping
steps.notes: Library helpers normalize macro settings and source table contracts.

steps.label: Read rows + overrides
steps.passes: lineage keys, source rows, custom values
steps.notes: Stable identity keeps overrides attached when source rows rematerialize.

steps.label: Render editable report
steps.passes: assembled rows, conflict flags, column schema
steps.notes: The same table engine renders cross-obra data with macro-specific controls.

flows.label: Generate document
flows.summary: A template draft hydrates data, renders a document, and stores the generated file as a normal obra document.

steps.label: Start draft
steps.passes: obra id, folder id, template id, input data
steps.notes: The route gives users an editable draft before generating the final file.

steps.label: Generate
steps.passes: template schema, hydrated aliases, draft values
steps.notes: The API owns document file naming and server-side rendering.

steps.label: Read template + obra data
steps.passes: template version, tenant, obra, configured inputs
steps.notes: Generated documents remain anchored to tenant, obra, folder, and template version.

steps.label: Save generated file
steps.passes: docx/pdf bytes and metadata
steps.notes: The output becomes a regular document in the obra file tree.

steps.label: Preview result
steps.passes: document URL, MIME type, metadata
steps.notes: Viewer components show the generated artifact alongside uploaded documents.

flows.label: Schedule reminder
flows.summary: A pendiente or due rule schedules workflow work that later writes notifications, calendar entries, or emails.

steps.label: Create pendiente
steps.passes: obra id, due rule, assignee, document context
steps.notes: Reminder routes store the operational obligation.

steps.label: Persist schedule
steps.passes: pendiente, schedule row, notification intent
steps.notes: Database rows are the durable queue for reminders.

steps.label: Dispatch async work
steps.passes: schedule id, tenant id, trigger time
steps.notes: The workflow engine handles delayed and retryable effects.

steps.label: Apply rules
steps.passes: event payload, recipient policy, delivery channels
steps.notes: Rules decide in-app, email, and calendar outcomes.

steps.label: Optional email
steps.passes: template, recipient, tenant context
steps.notes: Email delivery is one side effect of the notification pipeline.

steps.label: Render inbox
steps.passes: notification rows, pendientes, calendar events
steps.notes: The page merges active work items for the user.

flows.label: Open protected route
flows.summary: Route visibility and access checks resolve the same tenant membership and route requirements before rendering or serving data.

steps.label: Navigate
steps.passes: URL, cookies, Supabase session
steps.notes: Server components and route handlers resolve auth on demand.

steps.label: Resolve active tenant
steps.passes: active_tenant_id cookie, memberships, route requirements
steps.notes: No middleware.ts is used; tenant checks happen in request code.

steps.label: Read roles
steps.passes: user id, tenant id, custom role assignments
steps.notes: RLS and helper queries enforce tenant boundaries.

steps.label: Gate navigation
steps.passes: allowed route ids and sidebar sections
steps.notes: Sidebar visibility follows the same route-access config as server checks.

steps.label: Authorize API
steps.passes: membership and capability result
steps.notes: API routes reject forbidden actions even if a link is manually visited.

flows.label: Export PDF report
flows.summary: Report HTML is rendered server-side to a downloadable PDF using Puppeteer-compatible Chromium.

steps.label: Prepare report
steps.passes: macro rows, report config, filters
steps.notes: Report components build the printable HTML.

steps.label: POST render request
steps.passes: HTML payload, page size, tenant/obra metadata
steps.notes: The PDF route handles server rendering.

steps.label: Optional archive
steps.passes: PDF bytes and document metadata
steps.notes: Reports can be downloaded or stored depending on caller behavior.

flows.label: Seed demo tenant
flows.summary: Local scripts create a representative tenant, obras, defaults, documents, rows, and demo capabilities for development.

steps.label: Run pnpm seed:demo-tenant
steps.passes: environment variables and service role credentials
steps.notes: The package script starts the demo bootstrap module.

steps.label: Insert demo data
steps.passes: tenant, memberships, obras, tablas, rows, configs
steps.notes: Scripts use elevated credentials for controlled local setup.

steps.label: Upload fixtures
steps.passes: CSV/PDF/demo artifacts
steps.notes: Demo documents exercise file manager and extraction paths.

steps.label: Demo appears in app
steps.passes: tenant cookie targets seeded tenant
steps.notes: The app uses normal runtime paths after seeding.

flows.label: Next build
flows.summary: The production build compiles routes and shared packages into the Next.js output bundle.

steps.label: Run pnpm build
steps.passes: package script: next build --turbopack
steps.notes: The build script is defined in package.json.

steps.label: Compile routes
steps.passes: App Router pages, server components, route handlers
steps.notes: Next processes app routes and server/client boundaries.

steps.label: Bundle UI
steps.passes: React components, CSS, client component chunks
steps.notes: Shared component imports become client and server chunks as needed.

steps.label: Bundle handlers
steps.passes: route handlers and server-only libraries
steps.notes: API routes retain server runtime dependencies.

steps.label: Emit .next
steps.passes: server bundle, client assets, manifests
steps.notes: The output is what deployment and packaging flows consume.

flows.label: ToDesktop build
flows.summary: Desktop packaging starts from the Next production artifact, then an external ToDesktop configuration wraps the deployed/local app shell.

steps.label: Build web app
steps.passes: pnpm build / next build output
steps.notes: No ToDesktop config was found in this repo, so the known handoff is the Next artifact.

steps.label: Produce bundle
steps.passes: .next server/client output
steps.notes: The desktop layer depends on a stable production web build.

steps.label: Wrap app
steps.passes: app URL or local server command, icons, signing settings
steps.notes: These settings likely live outside this repository unless added later.

steps.label: Runtime data access
steps.passes: same Supabase environment variables and network requests
steps.notes: The packaged desktop app still talks to Supabase and APIs through the web runtime.
